vix.note: CBOE VIX via FMP index-quote (free tier); auto-activates vix_spike_regime when px>=28 or day>=30

macro: Monday relief rally: The S&P 500 closed +0.3% at 7,406 and the Nasdaq +0.86% at 25,930 on June 8 as chip stocks rebounded from Friday's 2.6% rout, which ended a nine-week win streak after May payrolls (172k) doubled consensus and revived Fed-hike bets. VIX collapsed 12% to 18.92, signaling cooler near-term fear. All eyes now turn to Wednesday's May CPI (April ran 3.8% YoY; consensus near 4.2%) ahead of the June 16-17 FOMC, where markets price roughly 99% no change but expect a hawkish tone under new Chair Warsh. Iran-Israel strikes and elevated crude keep a geopolitical and inflation tail risk live.
action: Hold positions into Wednesday's CPI print; Monday's bounce and the VIX drop are constructive, but a hot May reading above 4.2% would likely re-ignite the rate-driven semis selloff, so wait for the inflation data to clear before adding chip exposure (NVDA, SMH, AVGO).

notes.NVDA: Nvidia is doubling down on CPUs, expanding from server CPUs into the client CPU market, a fresh competitive threat to Intel and AMD. Cramer flags sovereign AI as a powerful new demand driver that reduces NVDA's hyperscaler dependence.
notes.AVGO: Broadcom broke its losing streak Monday after last week's weak-guidance-driven rout that dragged the whole AI-chip complex lower. Sentiment is stabilizing but the guidance overhang and high volatility remain.
notes.INTC: Nvidia's move into the client CPU market adds a new competitive threat to Intel's core x86 franchise on top of existing data-center share pressure.

long.h: Nvidia doubles down on CPUs, enters client CPU market; fresh threat to Intel and AMD
long.p: Nvidia expects strong server-CPU revenue this year and is moving into client CPUs too, widening its competitive front against the legacy x86 incumbents.

long.h: Stocks find relief as chips rebound; Broadcom breaks losing streak ahead of inflation data
long.p: Tech advanced Monday with the S&P 500 up 0.3% and Nasdaq up 0.86%. Broadcom snapped its slide that began on weak guidance, with May CPI due Wednesday.

long.h: Cramer: sovereign AI is Nvidia's powerful new growth driver, easing hyperscaler reliance
long.p: Governments are investing heavily in domestic AI infrastructure, creating chip demand less tied to near-term hyperscaler returns and diversifying NVDA's customer base.

long.h: Hot CPI report likely to keep Fed on guard; rate-hike odds creeping higher
long.p: With April CPI at 3.8% and May expected near 4.2%, a firm print would reinforce a hawkish hold at the June 16-17 FOMC and keep year-end hike risk on the table.

long.h: Stocks rebound, led by chips, as CPI data looms Wednesday and PPI Thursday
long.p: Markets steadied after Friday's selloff, but anything above expectations in this week's inflation prints could keep investors cautious rather than buying the dip.

short.h: Bitcoin steady above $63,000 as AI stocks rebound; BNB and SOL edge higher
short.p: BTC closed June 8 near $63,063 and held above $63k into Tuesday, trailing the equity rebound that was helped by an Nvidia-SK Hynix chip pact and a strong SpaceX IPO.

short.h: BTC at a CPI crossroads: rally toward $70k or slide back to $60k
short.p: The rebound from the $60k region has eased selling pressure, but Wednesday's CPI may decide whether buyers can build momentum or whether support gives way again.

short.h: Coinbase strategist: institutions are buying BTC near $60-65k, ETF and corporate demand firm
short.p: John D'Agostino says large investors prefer accumulating Bitcoin in weakness rather than chasing highs, suggesting the recent crash has not scared off institutional buyers.

short.h: Arca rejects Saylor's AI-rotation theory, blames Strategy's own 32 BTC sale for last week's crash
short.p: The debate over what drove the drawdown matters for positioning: a one-off forced sale is less bearish than a structural rotation out of crypto into AI equities.

short.h: Hayes warns higher oil could push Trump into anti-AI rhetoric, hitting stocks and Bitcoin together
short.p: A scenario where elevated crude pressures the administration to turn on the AI trade is a reminder that BTC and tech equities remain correlated risk assets in this regime.
